papers.title_en: Going Beyond the Edge: Distributed Inference of Transformer Models on Ultra-Low-Power Wireless Devices
papers.authors: Alexander Gräfe, Ding Huo, Johannes Berger, Marco Zimmerling, Sebastian Trimpe
papers.evidence: 多设备上的Transformer模型分布式推理
papers.tldr: 针对超低功耗物联网设备无法独立运行大型Transformer模型的问题，本文提出了CATS框架。该框架通过协同设计模型分区、无线通信和训练过程，实现了分布式推理。其核心是SomeGather通信原语，通过选择性广播激活列减少带宽和内存占用，并结合消息丢弃训练提高无线传输鲁棒性。实验显示，CATS支持多达16台设备协作，可运行比单机容量大14倍的模型。
papers.abstract_en: Transformer models are rapidly becoming a cornerstone of modern Internet of Things (IoT) applications, yet their computational and memory demands far exceed the capabilities of a single typical ultra-low-power IoT device. We present CATS, a framework for distributed transformer inference on ultra-low-power wireless devices, enabling multiple devices to collaboratively execute models far larger than what a single device can sustain. At its core, CATS is a communication-aware distributed transformer inference scheme co-designed across transformer partitioning, wireless communication and training. It employs SomeGather, a new pruned communication primitive that selectively broadcasts activation columns to reduce communication bandwidth and RAM usage without sacrificing model accuracy. Building on SomeGather, we design a partitioning method that exploits this primitive for efficient model parallelism. To cope with unreliable wireless communication, CATS employs message-dropout during training, which mimics packet losses and yields models that are robust to message loss during inference. In real-world experiments, we show that CATS brings distributed transformer inference to ultra-low-power wireless devices for the first time, with deployments on up to 16 devices that collaboratively execute transformer models up to 14 times larger than what a single device can run.

papers.title_en: PipeSD: An Efficient Cloud-Edge Collaborative Pipeline Inference Framework with Speculative Decoding
papers.authors: Yunhe Han, Yunqi Gao, Bing Hu, Mahdi Boloursaz Mashhadi, Yitong Duan, Pei Xiao, Yanfeng Zhang
papers.evidence: 具有隐私增强功能的云边协作大模型推理
papers.tldr: 针对云边协作的大模型推理，本文提出PipeSD框架。该框架利用投机解码技术，通过动态规划优化的令牌批处理流水线机制，实现了令牌生成与通信的重叠，并引入双阈值非自回归验证触发机制及贝叶斯优化自动调优器，解决了现有框架资源利用率低和验证触发不灵活的问题。实验表明，PipeSD在提升推理速度的同时显著降低了能耗。
papers.abstract_en: Speculative decoding can significantly accelerate LLM inference, especially given that its cloud-edge collaborative deployment offers cloud workload offloading, offline robustness, and privacy enhancement. However, existing collaborative inference frameworks with speculative decoding are constrained by (i) sequential token generation and communication with low resource utilization, and (ii) inflexible cloud non-autoregressive verification (NAV) triggering that induces premature verification or costly rollbacks. In this paper, we propose PipeSD, an efficient cloud-edge collaborative pipeline inference framework with speculative decoding. PipeSD overlaps token generation and communication by a token-batch pipeline scheduling mechanism optimized by dynamic programming, and improves verification flexibility through a dual-threshold NAV triggering mechanism with a lightweight Bayesian optimization autotuner. We implement PipeSD using llama-cpp-python, PyTorch, and FastAPI, and evaluate it on a real-world cloud-edge testbed with two draft-target model pairs across four scenarios. Results show that PipeSD consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines, achieving 1.16x-2.16x speedup and reducing energy consumption by 14.3%-25.3%.

papers.title_en: Beyond Controlled Noise: Achieving Symmetric FHE through Dynamic Position Shifting
papers.evidence: 用于安全分布式计算的对称全同态加密，无需复杂噪声管理
papers.tldr: 针对传统全同态加密（FHE）计算开销大和噪声管理复杂的问题，本文提出了一种基于明文分段和动态位置偏移的新型对称FHE方案。该方案在基础模运算加密之上，引入了双调节器系统（指数和系数调节器），通过动态重定向明文片段位置来抑制乘法过程中的噪声增长和密钥指数累积。研究通过绑定机制确保了安全性，为实现高效、低开销的同态运算提供了新思路。
papers.abstract_en: Traditional Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) schemes often suffer from prohibitive computational overhead and complex noise management. In this paper, we propose a novel symmetric FHE through a mechanism of plaintext fragmentation and dynamic interposition. Our approach is built upon a modular encryption foundation, c = mk + rp, which is naturally additive but typically limited by exponential noise growth during multiplication. To resolve this, we introduce an interposition framework where the plaintext is partitioned into multiple fragments across distinct logical positions. We introduce a dual-regulator system to govern the multiplication process; exponent regulators (t_i) redirect the product of fragments to a new target position, preventing the accumulation of secret key exponents, while coefficient regulators (d_i) normalize the resulting scalars. Security is established through a binding mechanism where exponents and coefficients are mutually dependent, shielding the secret key k from algebraic manipulation and substitution attacks.
